label: Bing Search
description: Search the web using Microsoft Bing Web Search API v7 hosted on Azure.

form.title: Connect Bing Search
form.subtitle: Paste your Azure Bing Search subscription key to start querying the web.

methods.label: Subscription Key
methods.description: Paste the subscription key from your Azure Bing Search resource. The fastest path — no OAuth app setup required.
methods.how_to:
## Get your Bing Search subscription key

1. Sign in to the [Azure Portal](https://portal.azure.com).
2. In the search bar at the top, type **Bing Search** and select **Bing Search** under Marketplace, or navigate to an existing Bing Search resource.
3. If you don't have a resource yet, click **Create** → choose a subscription, resource group, region, and pricing tier, then click **Review + create**.
4. Once the resource is deployed, open it and go to **Resource Management → Keys and Endpoint** in the left sidebar.
5. Copy either **Key 1** or **Key 2** (both are valid; Key 2 lets you rotate without downtime).
6. Paste the key into the **Subscription Key** field below.
7. Optionally note the **Endpoint** region shown on that same page and enter it in the **Azure Region** field (defaults to `global`).

## Notes

- Keys are 32-character hex strings. Do not share them — anyone with the key can use your quota.
- To rotate: generate a new key in the Azure Portal, update Anton, then delete the old key.
- Free-tier resources have a limited number of transactions per month. Upgrade the pricing tier in the Azure Portal if you hit quota limits.
- The Bing Search v7 API endpoint is `https://api.bing.microsoft.com/v7.0/search`.

fields.name: subscription_key
fields.label: Subscription Key
fields.description: Found in Azure Portal → your Bing Search resource → Keys and Endpoint. Either Key 1 or Key 2 works.

fields.name: azure_region
fields.label: Azure Region
fields.description: The market/region to bias results toward. Corresponds to the 'mkt' parameter. Leave as Global for worldwide results.

fields.name: custom_endpoint
fields.label: Custom Endpoint URL
fields.description: Override the default Bing Search v7 endpoint. Leave blank unless your Azure resource uses a custom subdomain or private endpoint.
